A later report of Carol's injuries would reveal that she sustained multiple broken bones and suffered severe blunt force trauma to the head, despite wearing a bicycle helmet at the time of her fall.
On March 17th, two months following Carol's death, 43-year-old Artisha Tisha Polk was arrested and placed on a $20,000 bond.
According to news reports, five days later, on March 22nd, 2024, Tisha and Kathy were both fired from Florida Drawbridges Incorporated.
Tisha initially pleaded not guilty to her charges and was facing 9 to 15 years in prison if convicted.
With the approval of Carol's family, Tisha ultimately agreed to accept a deal, pleading guilty to second-degree felony manslaughter.

She was sentenced to two days in jail, with two days credit for time served and placed on an eight-year probation, the terms of which included 200 hours of community service and a permanent ban from working at any job operating heavy machinery.
She was also ordered to write a letter of apology to Carol Wright's family.

However, in April of 2023, she was found in violation of this probation after a routine drug test came back positive for a controlled substance, drawing questions about the supposed drug test her supervisor Kathy claimed she passed after the incident.
After this violation, it was discovered she never began her community service and never wrote her letter of apology to Carol's family.
For violating the probation, additional conditions were added, including a curfew and a substance abuse evaluation.
According to news reports, Carol's family won an $8.2 million settlement against Florida Drawbridges Incorporated in July of 2022.
As part of the settlement agreement, the company instituted criminal background checks, new training for bridge operators, and routine audits without a various supervisors.
Additionally, all bridge tenders are now required to watch a 23-minute video on Carol Wright's life.
Kathy Harper was not charged with any crimes related to Carol Wright's death.
